message:
test(core): self-connection read-your-own-write under peer commits

Three integration tests that a single connection sees its own writes
even while peer connections commit on the same WAL/MVCC database:

- prepared_select_on_same_connection_survives_peer_commits_without_page_growth
- ad_hoc_query_on_same_connection_survives_peer_commits_without_page_growth
- same_connection_mixed_txn_read_your_own_write_never_returns_zero_rows

Shaped after the swarm_multiprocess regression surface — exercises
PRAGMA fsqlite.concurrent_mode=ON + WAL + concurrent inserts on negative
"hot rowid" ranges so peer commits touch the same b-tree leaf as the
reader's active cursor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
